title: Nectar Language Specification
description: Machine-readable specification for the Nectar programming language. Designed for AI code-generation systems to look up syntax, semantics, and idiomatic patterns.

impl.description: Add methods to a type or implement a trait

store.description: Declare a global reactive state store

agent.description: Declare an AI agent with tools and prompts

router.description: Declare a URL router mapping paths to components

mod.description: Declare a module (external file or inline block)

context: before fn, struct, enum, store, field
pub.description: Mark an item as publicly visible

let.description: Bind a value to a name (immutable by default)

mut.description: Make a binding or borrow mutable

signal.description: Declare a reactive variable that triggers re-renders on change

ref.description: Bind by reference in a pattern

in.description: Separates binding from iterator in for loops

return.description: Return a value from a function

yield.description: Emit a value from a stream

async.description: Mark a function or action as asynchronous

spawn.description: Run work on a background thread

stream.description: Iterate over async data as it arrives

suspend.description: Show fallback while async content loads

prompt.description: Define system prompt or create a prompt template

tool.description: Define a function the AI can invoke

route.description: Map a URL path to a component

fallback.description: Component to render when no route matches

render.description: Template block that produces the UI

animate.description: Trigger a named animation on an element

skeleton.description: Placeholder UI shown while data loads

action.description: Method that can mutate store state

effect.description: Side effect that runs when signal dependencies change

catch.description: Handle errors from a try block

Fragment.description: Group elements without a wrapper node

Program.description: Top-level program node containing all items

Function.syntax: pub fn name<'a, T>(param: Type) -> ReturnType where T: Trait { body }
Function.example: fn add(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32 { a + b }

Component.description: UI component — first-class reactive UI primitive

fields.name: String
fields.props: [Prop] — declared as parameters in parens after name

Component.syntax: component Name<T>(prop: Type = default) where T: Trait { state; methods; style {}; transition {}; skeleton {}; error_boundary {}; render {} }

RenderBlock.description: Template tree that produces DOM output

TemplateNode.description: A node in the template tree

StructDef.syntax: pub struct Name<'a, T> where T: Trait { pub field: Type, }
StructDef.example: struct User { id: u32, name: String, email: String }

EnumDef.description: Enum with variants that may carry data
EnumDef.syntax: pub enum Name<T> { Variant1, Variant2(Type), Variant3(T, U) }
EnumDef.example: enum AuthStatus { LoggedOut, Loading, LoggedIn(User), Error(String) }

ImplBlock.description: Add methods to a type, optionally implementing a trait
ImplBlock.syntax_trait: impl TraitName for TypeName { fn method(&self) { ... } }

TraitDef.description: Interface definition with optional default method bodies
TraitDef.syntax: trait Name<T> { fn method(&self) -> Type; fn with_default(&self) { ... } }

StoreDef.syntax: store Name { signal field: Type = value; action name(&mut self) { ... } computed name(&self) -> Type { expr } effect name(&self) { ... } }

ActionDef.syntax_sync: action name(&mut self, param: Type) { body }
ActionDef.syntax_async: async action name(&mut self, param: Type) { body }

EffectDef.description: Side effect that auto-runs when signal dependencies change

AgentDef.description: AI agent wrapping LLM interaction with tools and UI
AgentDef.syntax: agent Name { prompt system = "..."; signal field: Type = val; tool name(p: T) -> R { ... } fn method(&mut self) { ... } render { ... } }

ToolDef.description: Function exposed for AI to call

RouterDef.description: URL router mapping paths to components
RouterDef.syntax: router Name { route "/path" => Component, route "/path/:param" => Component guard { expr }, fallback => NotFoundComponent }

LazyComponentDef.description: Code-split component loaded on first render

SkeletonDef.description: Loading placeholder UI rendered during SSR and before data arrives

ErrorBoundary.description: Catches render errors and shows fallback UI

ModDef.description: Module declaration — external file or inline

Block.description: Sequence of statements; the last expression is the block's value

LetDestructure.syntax: let (a, b) = expr; / let Name { f, .. } = expr;

rules: Every value has exactly one owner
When the owner goes out of scope, the value is dropped
Ownership can be transferred (moved) via assignment
Values can be borrowed immutably (&) — multiple simultaneous borrows allowed
Values can be borrowed mutably (&mut) — only one mutable borrow at a time, no concurrent immutable borrows

generics.declaration: fn name<T>(...) or component Name<T>(...) or struct Name<T> { ... }

SelfExpr.description: Reference to current instance in methods

If.note: Produces a value — can be used in let bindings
If.example: let max = if a > b { a } else { b };

Block.note: Last expression is the block's value
Block.example: let r = { let x = 1; let y = 2; x + y };

Stream.syntax: for binding in stream source { body }
Stream.example: for chunk in stream fetch("https://api.example.com/stream") { process(chunk); }

Try.description: Unwrap Result/Option, propagate error on failure

Ident.description: Binds matched value to a name

component_structure.description: Full anatomy of a component declaration

sections.name: type_params
sections.location: angle brackets after name, before parens

sections.name: methods
sections.syntax: fn name(&self / &mut self, params) -> Type { body }

store_structure.description: Full anatomy of a store declaration

agent_structure.description: Full anatomy of an agent declaration

router_structure.description: Full anatomy of a router declaration
router_structure.full_example:
router AppRouter {
  route "/" => Home,
  route "/about" => About,
  route "/user/:id" => UserProfile,
  route "/settings" => Settings guard { AuthStore::is_logged_in() },
  route "/admin/*" => AdminPanel guard { AuthStore::is_admin() },
  fallback => NotFound,
}

counter_component.description: Basic stateful component with event handlers

async_data_loading.description: Component that fetches data with loading state

store_with_component.description: Global store consumed by a component

generic_component.description: Component with type parameters and trait bounds

full_app_skeleton.description: Complete app with router, store, and components
